title: blotiella coriacea
intro: Blotiella coriacea, also known as the African forest fern, is a type of epiphytic fern that grows in the tropical forests of West Africa. The medicinal properties of Blotiella coriacea have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. It is believed to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and fight off infections. The herbal preparations of Blotiella coriacea can be made into a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or it can be used as a poultice to apply directly to the skin.
